answer:
Let the number of apples be \(a\) and oranges be \(b\), with \(a > b\).  
Selling price of apples = ₹23 each with 15% profit, so cost price per apple = \( \frac{23}{1.15} = 20\).  
Selling price of oranges = ₹10 each with 25% profit, so cost price per orange = \( \frac{10}{1.25} = 8\).  
Total selling price: \(23a + 10b = 653\).  
Total cost price: \(20a + 8b\).  
Profit percentage \(z = \frac{653 - (20a + 8b)}{20a + 8b} \times 100\).  

Solving \(23a + 10b = 653\) with integer \(a > b\) gives \(a = 21, b = 17\).  
Cost price = \(20(21) + 8(17)
